subject: [PATCH v4] mm/vmpressure: skip socket pressure for costly order reclaim

2026-04-09.patch_summary: This patch modifies the vmpressure() function to skip socket pressure assertion when reclaim is triggered by high-order allocations on a fragmented system. The patch introduces an order-aware parameter that allows vmpressure() to differentiate between costly and non-costly orders, asserting socket pressure only for orders above PAGE_ALLOC_COSTLY_ORDER. This change aims to prevent unnecessary throttling of TCP throughput due to poor reclaim efficiency at high orders, while maintaining the existing behavior for memcg reclaim and low-priority reclaim scenarios.
